     1|---
     2|title: "Proxy Strain Analysis for China Market"
     3|params:
     4|  name: "05-proxy-strain-analysis"
     5|---
     6|
     7|# Proxy Strain Analysis for China Market
     8|
     9|## Setup
    10|
    11|```{r setup}
    12|#| label: setup
    13|params <- list(name = "05-proxy-strain-analysis")
    14|here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".qmd"), uuid = "e5f6a7b8-c9d0-1234-efgh-i1234567890")
    15|
    16|qproj::proj_create_dir_target(params$name)
    17|path_target <- qproj::path_target(params$name)
    18|path_source <- qproj::path_source(params$name)
    19|
    20|# Read upstream data
    21|path_01 <- qproj::path_source("01-public-data-mining")
    22|path_02 <- qproj::path_source("02-phage-host-prediction")
    23|path_04 <- qproj::path_source("04-synergy-scoring")
    24|```
    25|
    26|## Load Packages
    27|
    28|```{r packages}
    29|#| label: packages
    30|library(tidyverse)
    31|library(here)
    32|library(qproj)
    33|library(ape)
    34|library(ggplot2)
    35|library(ggtree)
    36|```
    37|
    38|## 1. Problem Statement
    39|
    40|### 1.1 The "Proxy Strain" Challenge
    41|
    42|**核心问题**：无法在德国获取中国本地病原体菌株，但需要在德国进行湿实验验证。
    43|
    44|**Notion 文档 Section 6.2**：
    45|- 由于无法获取中国本地病原体菌株，策略是识别**可获得的国际参考菌株**
    46|- 关键约束：代理菌株必须与中国本地菌株在基因组和表型上高度相似
    47|
    48|### 1.2 Target Pathogens for China Market
    49|
    50|| Pathogen | Disease | Target Animal | China Market Priority |
    51||----------|---------|---------------|----------------------|
    52|| *Clostridium perfringens* | 坏死性肠炎 | 家禽 (poultry) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
    53|| *Salmonella enterica* | 沙门氏菌病 | 家禽/牲畜 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
    54|| *Escherichia coli* (pathogenic) | 大肠杆菌病 | 家禽/水产 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
    55|| *Vibrio parahaemolyticus* | 急性肠胃炎 | 水产 (aquaculture) | ⭐⭐⭐ |
    56|| *Streptococcus suis* | 猪链球菌病 | 生猪 (pig) | ⭐⭐⭐ |
    57|
    58|## 2. Proxy Strain Selection Strategy
    59|
    60|### 2.1 Selection Criteria
    61|
    62|Based on Notion Research Master Plan:
    63|
    64|1. **Genomic similarity** > 95% ANI (Average Nucleotide Identity)
    65|2. **Phage susceptibility** (similar phage receptor profiles)
    66|3. **Availability** (can obtain from DSMZ, ATCC, etc.)
    67|4. **Safety** (moderate virulence for lab work)
    68|5. **Publication track record** (well-characterized in literature)
    69|
    70|### 2.2 Data Sources for Strain Comparison
    71|
    72|```{r data-sources}
    73|#| label: data-sources
    74|#| eval: false
    75|
    76|# Template: Search NCBI for available strains
    77|if (FALSE) {
    78|  library(rentrez)
    79|  
    80|  # Search for C. perfringens strains in NCBI BioSample
    81|  search_query <- 'Clostridium perfringens[Organism] AND "China"[Geolocation] AND ("strain" OR "isolate") AND ("genome" OR "complete genome")'
    82|  
    83|  strains_search <- rentrez::entrez_search(
    84|    db = "biosample",
    85|    term = search_query,
    86|    retmax = 100
    87|  )
    88|  
    89|  # Save for analysis
    90|  write_lines(
    91|    strains_search$ids,
    92|    file.path(path_target, "raw", "c_perfringens_china_strains.txt")
    93|  )
    94|}
    95|```
    96|
    97|## 3. Strain Comparison Analysis
    98|
    99|### 3.1 ANI Calculation Template
   100|
   101|```{r ani-template}
   102|#| label: ani-template
   103|#| eval: false
   104|
   105|# Template for ANI calculation using pyani (Python) or fastANI
   106|# Install: conda install -c bioconda pyani fastani
   107|
   108|ani_script <- c(
   109|  "#!/bin/bash",
   110|  "# ANI calculation script",
   111|  paste0("TARGET_DIR=", path_target, "/processed/ani_results"),
   112|  "mkdir -p $TARGET_DIR",
   113|  "",
   114|  "# Run fastANI",
   115|  "# fastANI -q query_genome.fasta -r reference_genome.fasta -o $TARGET_DIR/ani_output.txt",
   116|  "",
   117|  "# Batch ANI for multiple strains",
   118|  "# for q in strains/*.fasta; do",
   119|  "#   for r in reference/*.fasta; do",
   120|  "#     fastANI -q $q -r $r -o $TARGET_DIR/$(basename $q)_vs_$(basename $r).txt",
   121|  "#   done",
   122|  "# done"
   123|)
   124|
   125|write_lines(
   126|  ani_script,
   127|  file.path(path_target, "scripts", "run_ani.sh")
   128|)
   129|
   130|# Make executable
   131|Sys.chmod(file.path(path_target, "scripts", "run_ani.sh"), "755")
   132|```
   133|
   134|### 3.2 Phage Receptor Profile Comparison
   135|
   136|```{r receptor-comparison}
   137|#| label: receptor-comparison
   138|#| eval: false
   139|
   140|# Template: Compare phage receptor genes
   141|# Key receptors to check:
   142|# - LPS (Lipopolysaccharide) biosynthesis genes
   143|# - T4P (Type IV Pilus) genes
   144|# - Cell wall teichoic acid genes
   145|# - Capsular polysaccharide genes
   146|
   147|# Load genome annotations (from eggNOG-mapper or Prokka)
   148|# query_annotation <- read_tsv(file.path(path_02, "processed", "query_annotation.tsv"))
   149|# reference_annotation <- read_tsv(file.path(path_02, "processed", "reference_annotation.tsv"))
   150|
   151|# Extract receptor-related genes
   152|# receptor_genes <- c("lpx", "waa", "pil", "tat", "cap", "wzy")
   153|
   154|# Compare presence/absence
   155|# comparison_table <- bind_rows(
   156|#   query_annotation %>% mutate(type = "query"),
   157|#   reference_annotation %>% mutate(type = "reference")
   158|# ) %>%
   159|#   filter(grepl(paste(receptor_genes, collapse = "|"), gene_id, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
   160|#   group_by(type, gene_id) %>%
   161|#   summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop")
   162|
   163|# knitr::kable(comparison_table, caption = "Phage Receptor Gene Comparison")
   164|```
   165|
   166|## 4. Proxy Strain Candidates
   167|
   168|### 4.1 *Clostridium perfringens* Proxies
   169|
   170|*To be populated after analysis*
   171|
   172|| Strain | Origin | ANI to China Isolate | Phage Susceptibility | Availability | Score |
   173||--------|--------|---------------------|----------------------|--------------|-------|
   174|| ATCC 13124 | USA | - | Known | ATCC/DSMZ | - |
   175|| DSM 756 | Germany | - | Moderate | DSMZ | - |
   176|| TYPE B STRAIN | - | - | - | - | - |
   177|
   178|### 4.2 *Salmonella enterica* Proxies
   179|
   180|| Strain | Serovar | ANI to China Isolate | Phage Susceptibility | Availability | Score |
   181||--------|---------|---------------------|----------------------|--------------|-------|
   182|| ATCC 14028 | Typhimurium | - | High | ATCC | - |
   183|| DSM 554 | Enteritidis | - | Moderate | DSMZ | - |
   184|
   185|### 4.3 *Vibrio* spp. Proxies (Aquaculture)
   186|
   187|| Strain | Species | Origin | ANI | Host Range | Score |
   188||--------|---------|--------|-----|------------|-------|
   189|| ATCC 17802 | V. parahaemolyticus | USA | - | Known | - |
   190|| DSM 10027 | V. cholerae | Germany | - | Moderate | - |
   191|
   192|## 5. Experimental Validation Plan
   193|
   194|### 5.1 Proxy Validation Workflow
   195|
   196|Based on Notion document Section 7 (The Bioinformatics-First Strategy):
   197|
   198|**Step 1**: In silico validation
   199|- ANI > 95% to China isolates
   200|- Phage receptor profile match > 80%
   201|- Metabolic capability similarity
   202|
   203|**Step 2**: In vitro validation (Germany)
   204|- Phage isolation using proxy strain
   205|- Efficiency of plating (EOP) test
   206|- Lytic spectrum confirmation
   207|
   208|**Step 3**: Technology transfer to China
   209|- Send validated phages to China collaborator
   210|- Validate on local China isolates
   211|- Compare efficacy (German proxy vs. China local)
   212|
   213|### 5.2 Validation Metrics
   214|
   215|```{r validation-metrics}
   216|#| label: validation-metrics
   217|#| eval: false
   218|
   219|validation_criteria <- tibble(
   220|  metric = c("ANI similarity", "Receptor match", "Phage susceptibility", "Lytic spectrum", "Growth inhibition"),
   221|  threshold = c("> 95%", "> 80%", "> 90%", "> 5 strains", "> 2 log reduction"),
   222|  method = c("fastANI", "Gene presence/absence", "EOP assay", "Spot test", "In vitro co-culture")
   223|)
   224|
   225|knitr::kable(validation_criteria, caption = "Proxy Strain Validation Criteria")
   226|```
   227|
   228|## 6. Commercial Implications
   229|
   230|### 6.1 China Market Access Strategy
   231|
   232|From Notion document:
   233|- **Competing companies**: 青岛诺安百特 (Qingdao Nuo'anbai), CJ BIO (Korea)
   234|- **Strategy**: Use German lab for R&D, China partner for trials + commercialization
   235|- **Timeline**: Proxy validation (3 months) → China validation (6 months) → Market entry (12 months)
   236|
   237|### 6.2 IP Considerations
   238|
   239|- Patent strategy must cover **both** proxy and local strain applications
   240|- Phage cocktails should be broad enough to cover strain variations
   241|- Probiotic combinations need to be effective across strain spectra
   242|
   243|## 7. NotebookLM Validation
   244|
   245|### 7.1 Query for Evidence
   246|
   247|Use NotebookLM to validate proxy strain strategy:
   248|
   249|```bash
   250|nlm notebook query phage-synergy "What proxy strains are used for Clostridium perfringens research in Europe?"
   251|```
   252|
   253|### 7.2 Add Results to NotebookLM
   254|
   255|After completing proxy analysis, add to NotebookLM:
   256|
   257|```bash
   258|nlm source add phage-synergy --text "$(cat proxy_strain_report.txt)" --title "Proxy Strain Analysis pr0117"
   259|```
   260|
   261|## 8. Session Info
   262|
   263|```{r session-info}
   264|#| label: session-info
   265|sessionInfo()
   266|```
   267|
   268|## 9. Next Steps
   269|
   270|1. **Complete ANI analysis** for top candidate strains
   271|2. **Validate phage receptor profiles** against literature
   272|3. **Order proxy strains** from DSMZ/ATCC
   273|4. **Design validation experiments** (Germany lab)
   274|5. **Proceed to Module 06** (`06-mechanism-exploration.qmd`)
   275|
   276|---
   277|*Generated by qproj workflow for pr0117-PhageProbioticSynergy*
   278|
